overview: Advance the simulation from deterministic pressure maps into richer district strategy, scenario comparison, and workflow automation while preserving prstack as the operating surface.
goals: Add district strategy layers that make scenarios diverge in more meaningful ways
Expose simulation comparison and replay views in the frontend
Keep Ralph loop usage bounded and gated by prstack review and qa
nonGoals: No remote PR automation until the repository has a real remote workflow
successMetrics: A single bounded Ralph iteration can complete one self-contained story and pass prstack review/qa
The frontend can compare at least two saved or replayed simulation states
District strategies materially alter simulation metrics and reports
openQuestions: Should the long-term world model stay tile-first or move to district-first with tile overlays?

stack.simulation: Custom deterministic engine with replay and persistence

routes.name: Simulation Console
routes.purpose: Run scenarios, interventions, saves, and replay

uiNotes: Keep the current bold editorial visual style
Do not regress save/load, replay rail, or intervention controls
rules: Every story must pass prstack review and prstack qa

stories.title: Add district strategy modifiers
stories.description: As a planner, I want districts to have explicit strategy modes so different districts evolve in distinct ways.
acceptanceCriteria: Add district-level strategy definitions that affect runtime behavior beyond generic pressure scalars
Example: a growth-first district increases access or land value growth more aggressively than a resilience-first district
Negative case: a district without a strategy still runs using safe defaults
Update backend tests to cover the new strategy behavior

stories.title: Build state comparison panel
stories.description: As a user, I want to compare replay or saved states so I can see what changed between two points in time.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a frontend comparison panel for at least two replayed or saved states
Example: compare tick 0 vs tick 4 and show differences in population, land value, and urban cells
Negative case: when fewer than two states are selected, the comparison panel should not crash and should show a clear empty state
If new UI logic is added, keep npm run build passing

stories.title: Add bounded Ralph loop reporting into prstack
stories.description: As a developer, I want prstack loop runs to record Ralph outputs in a predictable place so autonomous iterations stay inspectable.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist Ralph loop summaries or references under ops/prstack/state/reports or a clearly linked location
Example: a no-commit Ralph run records which story was selected and where logs were written
Negative case: if Ralph is unavailable or fails, prstack reports the error without corrupting workflow state
